_comment: OpenObserve alert rules — bootstrap source of truth.
_lineage: Consumed by scripts/setup-monitoring.py which POSTs each rule to OpenObserve's /api/{org}/alerts endpoint. Each rule references a monitor by name. Idempotent — re-runs upsert by 'name'.
_routing_strategy: Every alert fires to a single Keep webhook with severity in the JSON payload. Keep's workflows.yml then routes warning → #grove-alerts-warning, critical → #grove-alerts-critical with @here. No Slack/PagerDuty wiring exists in OpenObserve — that's all Keep's job.

_destinations.name: keep-webhook
_destinations._secret_substitution: {webhook_token} is replaced by setup-monitoring.py from KEEP_WEBHOOK_TOKEN env at bootstrap time.

alerts.name: frontend-down-warning
alerts.trigger_condition: status != 2xx for >60s OR response time >5s for >2 checks
alerts._message_template: [{tenant}] {route} degraded — status={status_code} duration={duration_ms}ms. Runbook: docs/RUNBOOKS.md#frontend-degraded

alerts.name: frontend-down-critical
alerts.trigger_condition: monitor unreachable (timeout or connection refused) for >30s
alerts._message_template: [{tenant}] FRONTEND DOWN — root endpoint unreachable for {duration_s}s. Runbook: docs/RUNBOOKS.md#frontend-down

alerts.name: odoo-down-critical
alerts._message_template: ODOO BACKEND DOWN — /web/login unreachable for {duration_s}s. All storefront cart + checkout flows will fail. Runbook: docs/RUNBOOKS.md#odoo-down

alerts.name: odoo-degraded-warning
alerts.trigger_condition: status != 2xx,303 for >60s OR response time >10s for >3 checks
alerts._message_template: Odoo degraded — status={status_code} duration={duration_ms}ms. Storefronts may show empty product lists. Runbook: docs/RUNBOOKS.md#odoo-degraded

alerts.name: ghost-down-warning
alerts._message_template: [{tenant}] Ghost admin API unreachable — /blog page will fail. Runbook: docs/RUNBOOKS.md#ghost-down
alerts._note: Ghost is content-only; degraded Ghost doesn't break commerce, hence warning not critical.

alerts.name: postgres-down-critical
alerts._message_template: POSTGRES DOWN — TCP probe to postgres:5432 failed for {duration_s}s. Odoo will be unable to serve any request. Runbook: docs/RUNBOOKS.md#postgres-down

alerts.name: ssl-expiring-warning
alerts._message_template: [{host}] SSL cert expires in {days_remaining} days. Runbook: docs/RUNBOOKS.md#ssl-expiring
alerts._deferred: Local has no real certs. SSL monitors will fail-to-evaluate locally; alert is meaningful only once M2 droplet exists with real certs.

alerts.name: synthetic-health-critical
alerts._message_template: SYNTHETIC health failing — grove_headless /health is down (Odoo API unreachable or module unloaded). All storefront commerce fails. Runbook: docs/RUNBOOKS.md#odoo-down
alerts._note: Tier-1 synthetic (Hurl). Queries the synthetic_journey_success metric stream emitted by the synthetic-runner, not a built-in monitor.

alerts.name: synthetic-cart-critical
alerts.trigger_condition: journey='cart-flow' success==0 for >2 consecutive runs (any tenant)
alerts._message_template: [{tenant}] SYNTHETIC cart-flow failing — add-to-cart → cart-get broke. The money path is degraded. Runbook: docs/RUNBOOKS.md#cart-broken

alerts.name: synthetic-catalog-warning
alerts.trigger_condition: journey='catalog' success==0 for >2 consecutive runs (any tenant)
alerts._message_template: [{tenant}] SYNTHETIC catalog failing — product list/detail returned no usable data. Storefront may show empty shop. Runbook: docs/RUNBOOKS.md#catalog-empty

alerts.name: synthetic-latency-warning
alerts._message_template: [{tenant}] SYNTHETIC {journey} slow — p95 {duration_ms}ms over 5m (threshold 3000ms). Backend degrading. Runbook: docs/RUNBOOKS.md#synthetic-slow

alerts.name: synthetic-checkout-critical
alerts.trigger_condition: journey='checkout-canary' success==0 for >2 consecutive runs (any tenant)
alerts._message_template: [{tenant}] SYNTHETIC checkout-canary failing — the /orders bearer endpoint or the access_token gate is broken. Orders cannot be created. Runbook: docs/RUNBOOKS.md#checkout-broken
alerts._note: Only fires when SYNTHETIC_CANARY_ENABLED=true (the canary journey runs). Tests the money-path write + the PII access_token gate end-to-end.

alerts.name: cost-budget-warning
alerts._message_template: COST — month-to-date spend ${mtd} is >80% of the ${budget}/mo budget. Review the rightsizing dashboard. Runbook: docs/RUNBOOKS.md#cost-budget
alerts._note: CostOps (DO billing bridge). Threshold uses COST_MONTHLY_BUDGET from .env.monitoring; disabled when budget=0. Only fires when COST_BRIDGE_ENABLED=true.

alerts.name: cost-budget-critical
alerts._message_template: COST — month-to-date spend ${mtd} has EXCEEDED the ${budget}/mo budget. Runbook: docs/RUNBOOKS.md#cost-budget

alerts.name: cost-anomaly-warning
alerts.trigger_condition: day-over-day cost_account_month_to_date_usage delta > 2x trailing-7d avg daily delta
alerts._message_template: COST — daily spend spiked >2x baseline (a resource was scaled/added?). Check cost_resource_monthly_estimate for the mover. Runbook: docs/RUNBOOKS.md#cost-anomaly

alerts.name: synthetic-ghost-content-warning
alerts.trigger_condition: journey='ghost-content' success==0 for >2 consecutive runs (any tenant)
alerts._message_template: [{tenant}] SYNTHETIC ghost-content failing — Ghost Content API returned no posts (key rotated/invalid or blog empty). The tenant /blog page will fail. Runbook: docs/RUNBOOKS.md#ghost-content-empty
alerts._note: Only fires when SYNTHETIC_GHOST_ENABLED=true. Content-level depth beyond the ghost-*-admin availability monitors; warning (Ghost is content-only, doesn't break commerce).

alerts.name: droplet-cpu-warning
alerts._message_template: USE — droplet CPU >70% for 10m. Identify the hot container via docker_stats. Runbook: docs/RUNBOOKS.md#droplet-cpu
alerts._note: USE metric from the OTel Collector (hostmetrics). Utilization is a 0-1 fraction. Stream/field name pending live validation.

alerts.name: droplet-cpu-critical
alerts._message_template: USE — droplet CPU >90% for 10m. Imminent thrash risk. Restart non-critical containers or scale. Runbook: docs/RUNBOOKS.md#droplet-cpu

alerts.name: container-ram-warning
alerts._message_template: USE — [{container_name}] memory >80% of limit for 5m. Odoo workers most likely. Runbook: docs/RUNBOOKS.md#container-ram

alerts.name: container-ram-critical
alerts._message_template: USE — [{container_name}] memory >95% of limit — OOM kill imminent. Restart it. Runbook: docs/RUNBOOKS.md#container-ram

alerts.name: disk-data-warning
alerts._message_template: USE — data filesystem >75% full. MinIO objects or Postgres WAL growing. Clean up or add volume. Runbook: docs/RUNBOOKS.md#disk-data

alerts.name: disk-data-critical
alerts._message_template: USE — data filesystem >90% full — near-full kills MinIO writes + Postgres WAL. Immediate action. Runbook: docs/RUNBOOKS.md#disk-data
alerts._note: Tighter than typical (75/90) because MinIO + Postgres WAL fail silently-then-catastrophically when the volume fills. postgres-connections + the RED latency/5xx alerts need the postgresql receiver + Beyla (follow-ups).

alerts.name: odoo-orders-latency-warning
alerts._message_template: RED — Odoo /orders p95 >3s over 5m. Check Postgres connections + slow query log. Runbook: docs/RUNBOOKS.md#odoo-latency
alerts._note: RED metric from Beyla eBPF (Odoo). Metric stream/field names (http_server_request_duration, url_path) pending live validation on first Beyla run.

alerts.name: odoo-orders-latency-critical
alerts._message_template: RED — Odoo /orders p95 >8s — orders timing out. Check Postgres locks; restart Odoo if no DB cause. Runbook: docs/RUNBOOKS.md#odoo-latency

alerts.name: odoo-products-latency-warning
alerts._message_template: RED — Odoo /products p95 >1s. Cache miss or Postgres scan; check KeyDB hit rate. Runbook: docs/RUNBOOKS.md#odoo-latency

alerts.name: odoo-error-rate-warning
alerts._message_template: RED — Odoo 5xx rate >1% over 5m. Check Odoo worker logs for Python exceptions. Runbook: docs/RUNBOOKS.md#odoo-error-rate
alerts._note: Beyla RED. nextjs-* latency/5xx alerts need Next.js instrumentation.ts (grove-sites) — separate PR.

alerts.name: postgres-connections-warning
alerts._message_template: USE — Postgres connections >70% of max. Check for idle-in-transaction queries; likely an Odoo worker leak. Runbook: docs/RUNBOOKS.md#postgres-connections
alerts._note: USE metric from the OTel Collector `postgresql` receiver — OFF until a read-only pg_monitor user + POSTGRES_* env are set and the receiver is added to the metrics pipeline (see otel/README.md). Stream/field names pending live validation.

alerts.name: postgres-connections-critical
alerts._message_template: USE — Postgres connections >90% of max — new connections will be refused. Restart Odoo to clear leaked connections. Runbook: docs/RUNBOOKS.md#postgres-connections
